brief.decision_question: Which model should I trust for client memos?
brief.success_criteria: At least 80% similarity to the analyst summary.

recommendation: Mock · good (mock_good) — passed 5/5 (100%), avg latency 57ms, est. cost $0.00, local.

failure_cases.input_text: Q3 revenue reached $48.2M, up 22% year over year, driven by enterprise seat expansion. Net revenue retention held at 118%. Gross margin improved to 79% as infrastructure costs were renegotiated.
failure_cases.expected_text: Revenue grew 22% to $48.2M on enterprise expansion, with 118% net retention and margins improving to 79%.
failure_cases.output_text: This document discusses various financial topics and market conditions.

failure_cases.input_text: The company burned $6.1M in the quarter against $52M cash on hand, giving roughly eight quarters of runway. Management paused hiring outside of engineering and reaffirmed a path to breakeven by late next year.
failure_cases.expected_text: Burn of $6.1M leaves about eight quarters of runway; hiring paused except engineering, with breakeven targeted late next year.
failure_cases.output_text: This document discusses various financial topics and market conditions.

failure_cases.input_text: Churn ticked up to 4.2% monthly among self-serve customers, concentrated in accounts under ten seats. Enterprise churn remained under 1%. The team is shifting acquisition spend toward larger accounts. Management is testing onboarding fixes.
failure_cases.expected_text: Self-serve churn rose to 4.2% in small accounts while enterprise stayed under 1%, prompting a shift to larger-account acquisition.

failure_cases.input_text: A new competitor launched an aggressively priced tier and captured several mid-market logos. The company responded with annual-contract discounts, protecting retention but compressing average selling price by 9%.
failure_cases.expected_text: Competitive pricing pressure cost mid-market logos; annual discounts defended retention but cut average selling price 9%.
failure_cases.output_text: This document discusses various financial topics and market conditions.

failure_cases.input_text: The founders are raising a $15M Series B at a $90M post-money valuation, a 2.1x markup from the prior round. Lead investor is requesting a board seat and a standard 1x non-participating liquidation preference.
failure_cases.expected_text: Raising $15M Series B at $90M post (2.1x markup); lead seeks a board seat and a 1x non-participating preference.
failure_cases.output_text: This document discusses various financial topics and market conditions.
